description_long: This object is a vintage-style electric coffee maker, likely from Braun, featuring a cylindrical upper body in a muted orange color with subtle ventilation slits and a small rectangular switch. The lower section houses a transparent glass carafe with a black plastic handle and lid, sitting on a matching orange base that appears to contain an integrated heating plate. A prominent silver metallic handle structure runs vertically along the side, providing support and ease of handling. The power cord is black and extends from the base, ending in a two-prong plug, indicating its electrical operation. The overall design is utilitarian with minimal decorative elements, focusing on functionality and ease of use.
description: A vintage orange electric coffee maker with a glass carafe, black handle, and integrated heating base, featuring a visible power cord and minimalistic design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the glass carafe, black handle, and the Braun logo on the orange upper body, with the power cord visible at the base.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the carafe's spout, the black handle, and the power cord extending from the base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the metallic support handle structure and ventilation slits on the orange body, with the carafe partially obscured.
description_view_3: Back-side view focusing on the metallic handle structure, ventilation slits, and the power cord trailing away from the base.
